title: Workspace Enforcement & Final Go/No-Go v6
decision_rationale: GL-221 confirms tenant-scoped Developer Preview and controlled synthetic/demo review may continue, but workspace enforcement is reserved/nullable and not production-enforced. Production SaaS, real customer data, private grant/institutional data, official SDK/package, compliance certification, and live PostgreSQL production readiness remain NO-GO.

current_workspace_enforcement_state_summ.tenant_id: server-derived and application-layer enforced for primary and GL-215 secondary routes

workspace_enforcement_gap_assessment.mitigation: do not trust client workspace values
workspace_enforcement_gap_assessment.remaining_work: derive workspace from authenticated membership or trusted validated provider claim
add workspace ownership predicates and tests

workspace_enforcement_gap_assessment.mitigation: tenant-visible execution prechecks protect HTTP entry points
workspace_enforcement_gap_assessment.remaining_work: persist and enforce workspace ownership across derived records

implemented_hardening_summary: No backend source hardening was implemented. GL-221 adds conservative documentation, JSON artifact, local-only gate, and tests because real workspace enforcement requires broader schema, membership, and authorization work.
workspace_id_derivation_trust_model: No trusted workspace_id derivation exists. Future workspace context must be server-derived from operator membership or trusted validated IdP claims, never arbitrary headers or request bodies.
tenant_workspace_relationship_model: Tenant is the current enforced boundary. Workspace is reserved nullable metadata. Production requires each workspace to belong to exactly one tenant and each customer-owned resource to carry tenant plus workspace ownership.
unsafe_workspace_override_prevention: Current business route resource ownership ignores caller-supplied tenantId/workspaceId. No partial workspace override mechanism was added.
cross_workspace_lookup_denial_posture: Not claimed. Cross-tenant lookup denial exists; cross-workspace denial requires future workspace-scoped filtering.
cross_workspace_mutation_denial_posture: Not claimed. Cross-tenant mutation denial exists; cross-workspace mutation denial requires future workspace ownership predicates.
workspace_propagation_audit_evidence_pro: Audit has nullable workspace_id storage, but current trusted propagation is tenant-focused. Evidence, provenance, auditor, and compliance routes are tenant-prechecked by execution visibility but not independently workspace-enforced.
admin_operator_workspace_boundary: Admin/operator control plane preserves tenant assignment and safe audit events but does not implement workspace membership or workspace admin workflow.
demo_synthetic_workspace_boundary: Demo and synthetic flows remain bounded to Developer Preview / Controlled Preview and are not approved for real data.
controlled_external_review_workspace_bou: Controlled external technical review remains GO with strict boundaries, synthetic/demo data only, no public export unless a separate gate approves it.
production_readiness_impact: Workspace enforcement remains a blocker before Production SaaS, real customer data, private grant/institutional data, and production multi-tenant claims.
controlled_preview_impact: Developer Preview and controlled synthetic/demo review can continue under existing tenant-derived controls and safety boundaries.
iam_identity_decision_after_gl219: Improved controlled-preview posture; external IdP/OAuth/OIDC/JWT production readiness is not claimed.
runtime_infrastructure_decision_after_gl: Improved documentation and local gates; real production infrastructure readiness is not claimed.
public_export_decision_after_gl218: Separate-gate only; no export, public push, public publish, or visibility change is authorized by GL-221.

remaining_blockers.mitigation: keep Developer Preview / Controlled Preview only
remaining_blockers.remaining_work: complete production IAM, workspace, DB, ops, compliance gates
data governance, legal/security approval, production controls
workspace identity, membership, ownership, query/mutation enforcement

compact_next_roadmap: GL-222 workspace identity, membership, and ownership plan
GL-223 workspace-scoped lookup and mutation enforcement
GL-225 production identity provider readiness gate
GL-226 live PostgreSQL production readiness gate
findings: workspace_id remains reserved/nullable and not production-enforced
cross-workspace lookup and mutation denial are not claimed
no backend source change was safe within GL-221 scope
recommended_next_issues: GL-223 Workspace-scoped lookup and mutation enforcement
GL-225 Production identity provider readiness gate
GL-226 Live PostgreSQL production readiness gate
